perturbation.original_text: You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential and will not disclose the Evaluation Materials, in any manner, in whole or in part; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may only be disclosed to those of your directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "your representatives") who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between you and the Company (it being understood that your representatives shall be informed by you of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by you and shall agree to treat such information confidentially in accordance with this Agreement) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials confidential and will not disclose the Evaluation Materials, in any manner, in whole or in part; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may only be disclosed to those of your directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "your representatives") who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between you and the Company and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'completely' from 'completely confidential', it reduces the strength of the protection that was being provided. This will cause a conflict with page 1 in the statement 'You recognize and acknowledge the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: You recognize and acknowledge the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Evaluation Materials will be kept 'completely confidential'. The changed text removes the word 'completely', weakening the confidentiality obligation. This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which emphasizes the 'competitive value' of the Evaluation Materials and the 'damage that could result' from unauthorized use or disclosure. The weakened confidentiality standard in the changed text creates a direct conflict with the level of protection required by the Contradicted Text, making the enforceability of the agreement ambiguous and causing a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: RETURN OF DOCUMENTS Upon the Company's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Materials, you and your representatives will continue to be bound by your obligations pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: RETURN OF DOCUMENTS Upon the Company's reasonable request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Materials, you and your representatives will continue to be bound by your obligations pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Adding the word "reasonable" makes the request subject to interpretation, introducing ambiguity. The company can only make a reasonable request. By adding the term, the original agreement says it's upon the Company's request, regardless of the reason, to the new modified agreement, the Company's request has to be deemed 'reasonable'. This leads to ambiguity
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the Company's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'Upon the Company's request'. The changed text modifies this to 'Upon the Company's reasonable request'. The contradicted text states 'Upon the Company's request', meaning the contradicted text does not contain the word 'reasonable'. The insertion of 'reasonable' introduces a subjective element that did not exist before. The original text implies any request by the Company must be followed, while the changed text means the request must be deemed 'reasonable'. This introduces ambiguity. The contradiction lies in whether any request by the company has to be followed (original) or only if that request is deemed reasonable(changed).

perturbation.original_text: Without the Company's prior written consent, the persons who are involved in the possible transaction will not for a period of two years from the date of this Agreement directly or indirectly solicit for employment any person who is now employed by the Company (or whose activities are dedicated to the Company) in an executive or management level position or otherwise considered by the Company to be a key employee.
perturbation.changed_text: Without the Company's prior written consent, the persons who are involved in the possible transaction will not for a period of two years from the date of this Agreement directly solicit for employment any person who is now employed by the Company (or whose activities are dedicated to the Company) in an executive or management level position or otherwise considered by the Company to be a key employee.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the term "or indirectly" from the original text, means there is now ambiguity if the other party, does the action through an indirect manner, if it would be a breach or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without the Company's prior written consent, the persons who are involved in the possible transaction will not for a period of two years from the date of this Agreement directly or indirectly solicit for employment any person who is now employed by the Company (or whose activities are dedicated to the Company) in an executive or management level position or otherwise considered by the Company to be a key employee.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibits both direct and indirect solicitation of employees. The changed text removes 'or indirectly,' meaning the agreement is now unclear on whether indirect solicitation is prohibited. The contradicted text prohibits both direct and indirect solicitation. This introduces an ambiguity and inconsistency between the changed text and the contradicted text because it is now uncertain if indirect solicitation is a breach of contract, thus causing a contradiction as specified by the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: STANDSTILL You agree that until one year from the date of this Agreement, you will not without the prior approval of the Board of Directors of the Company (i) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a "group" (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or
perturbation.changed_text: STANDSTILL You agree that until one year from the date of this Agreement, you will not without the prior approval of the Board of Directors of the Company (i) acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a "group" (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or
perturbation.explanation: By removing "or make any proposal to acquire" there is ambiguity if a proposal is made but not directly acquired, if it's a breach or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: STANDSTILL You agree that until one year from the date of this Agreement, you will not without the prior approval of the Board of Directors of the Company (i) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a "group" (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits making *or* acquiring any proposal. The changed text removes "or make any proposal to acquire". The contradicted text states "acquire or make any proposal to acquire". The change causes ambiguity about whether a proposal without acquisition is a breach. Therefore, the changed text contradicts the contradicted text, creating a contradiction.
